userStories.title: Auto-detect project stack and pre-fill config.json quality commands
userStories.description:
When nax init runs, scan the project root for stack indicators and auto-fill quality.commands in nax/config.json.

Detection logic (in priority order):
1. bun.lockb or bunfig.toml -> runtime=bun
2. package-lock.json or yarn.lock or pnpm-lock.yaml -> runtime=node
3. tsconfig.json -> language=typescript
4. turbo.json -> monorepo=turborepo
5. pyproject.toml or setup.py -> language=python
6. Cargo.toml -> language=rust
7. go.mod -> language=go

Mapping to quality.commands:
- bun+ts -> { typecheck: bun run tsc --noEmit, lint: bun run lint, test: bun test }
- node+ts -> { typecheck: npx tsc --noEmit, lint: npm run lint, test: npm test }
- python -> { lint: ruff check ., test: pytest }
- rust -> { typecheck: cargo check, lint: cargo clippy, test: cargo test }
- go -> { typecheck: go vet ./..., lint: golangci-lint run, test: go test ./... }

Also detect linter: biome.json/biome.jsonc -> biome check, eslint config -> eslint.

Create new file src/cli/init-detect.ts with:
- detectProjectStack(projectRoot: string): ProjectStack
- buildQualityCommands(stack: ProjectStack): QualityCommands
- buildInitConfig(stack: ProjectStack): object

Integrate into initProject() in src/cli/init.ts: replace MINIMAL_PROJECT_CONFIG with buildInitConfig(detectProjectStack(projectRoot)).

Print detection result. If nothing detected, fall back to minimal config.
acceptanceCriteria: detectProjectStack correctly identifies bun+ts, node+ts, python, rust, go stacks
buildQualityCommands returns correct commands for each stack variant
nax init creates config.json with pre-filled quality.commands when stack detected
nax init falls back to minimal config when no stack detected
Biome and ESLint detected and used in lint commands when present
Unit tests cover all detection combinations

userStories.title: Context.md generation: template by default, LLM-powered with --ai flag
userStories.description:
Add context.md generation to nax init. Two modes:

Default mode (no flag): Generate a structured template context.md from filesystem scan only -- zero LLM cost.

AI mode (nax init --ai): Same scan, but pass results to an LLM to generate a richer narrative context.md.

Scan phase (both modes, no LLM, pure filesystem):
1. File tree: find . -type f excluding node_modules/.git/dist, head -200
2. Package manifest: read package.json (name, description, scripts, dependencies) or equivalent
3. Existing docs: read first 100 lines of README.md if present
4. Entry points: detect src/index.ts, src/main.ts, main.go, src/lib.rs, etc.
5. Config files: list tsconfig.json, biome.json, turbo.json, .env.example (names only)

Template mode (default):
- Build structured markdown template with sections pre-filled from scan data
- Sections left as TODO comments where scan data is insufficient
- Print: Generated nax/context.md template from project scan.

AI mode (--ai flag):
- Build prompt with scan results for LLM
- Use routing system cheapest available tier (fast/haiku-class)
- Token budget: ~3-5K input, ~1-2K output max
- Use existing nax LLM infrastructure -- do NOT hardcode a model
- If LLM call fails, fall back to template mode with warning

Both modes: write to nax/context.md. If exists, skip unless --force.

New file: src/cli/init-context.ts with:
- scanProject(projectRoot: string): ProjectScan
- generateContextWithLLM(scan, config): Promise<string>
- generateContextTemplate(scan): string
- initContext(projectRoot, options): Promise<void>
acceptanceCriteria: nax init generates nax/context.md template from filesystem scan (no LLM)
Template includes project name, detected stack, directory tree, entry points
nax init --ai calls LLM to generate richer context.md
LLM failure falls back to template mode with warning
Existing context.md is not overwritten without --force
Unit tests for scanProject and generateContextTemplate

userStories.title: Post-init checklist and unified init flow
userStories.description:
After nax init completes all setup steps, print a clear summary and next-steps checklist.

Unified flow for nax init (update initProject()):
1. Create nax/ directory structure (existing)
2. Detect project stack -> write config.json with quality commands (INIT-001)
3. Generate context.md template from scan (INIT-002); if --ai flag passed, use LLM
4. Write constitution.md with stack-aware defaults (enhance with detected stack)
5. Create hooks/ directory (existing)
6. Create templates/ via promptsInitCommand (existing)
7. Update .gitignore (add nax.lock, nax/**/runs/, nax/metrics.json)
8. Print summary

Summary shows all created files and next steps (review context.md, review config.json, nax generate, nax plan, nax run).

Enhance constitution.md generation:
- bun detected -> include Bun-native APIs guidance
- typescript detected -> include strict TypeScript guidance
- python detected -> include PEP 8 and type hints
- turbo detected -> include monorepo package boundaries

Update NAX_GITIGNORE_ENTRIES to include nax.lock, nax/**/runs/, nax/metrics.json.
acceptanceCriteria: nax init prints summary with all created files listed
constitution.md includes stack-specific conventions when stack detected
--ai flag is wired through to context generation
Unit tests verify summary output and .gitignore entries
